what_it_does: Defines a single HTML screen that loads shared styles and runtime scripts, declares a PIECE data object for a focused piece view, and mounts DNA.renderPiece(PIECE) into the page.
observations: The body contains only a mount element and scripts.
The PIECE object carries back text, preview content, take states, dial values, pin list content, an action label, and rail state.
The preview includes a super label, title, three CSS-variable plates, and three numbered positioned pins.
Inline comments state that this is frozen scr-piece content composed from data through DNA.renderPiece.
The page loads Google Fonts plus /surface styles and runtime scripts before rendering.
standouts: All screen content is concentrated in a single data literal consumed by a renderer.
The file explicitly presents live composition as proof for a per-archetype data-driven model.
conventions: Uses a minimal HTML shell with a runtime-driven mount point.
Keeps page-specific content in a constant named PIECE.
Represents selected state with compact fields such as on and railActive.
Stores pin layout as inline style strings inside the data.
Uses CSS custom properties for preview plate colors.
concerns: DNA.renderPiece is assumed to exist with no local guard or fallback.
Pin positions mix content data with presentation through raw style strings.
External font loading can affect rendering if the network dependency is unavailable.
notes: This file is a data-authored live rendering of a focused piece screen.
questions: What exact contract does DNA.renderPiece expect for the PIECE object?
Are the pin style strings intended as stable data or temporary composition hints?
What does railActive index 2 correspond to in the rendered rail?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file composes a frozen-style piece screen from a data object through a renderer.
intention_signals: It appears to demonstrate that a structurally distinct piece screen can be represented as data and rendered through a reusable archetype-specific renderer.
novelty: The file treats an entire focused-screen composition, including visual marks and navigation state, as a compact inline data payload.
contribution: Contributes a data schema example for a focused piece view, including preview bands, annotation pins, take switching, dials, action copy, and rail selection.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML fixture that defines a focused piece screen as inline PIECE data and renders it into a mount node with DNA.renderPiece.
intention_for_embedding: It was made to show a frozen piece screen being live-composed from data through an archetype renderer.
